rationale: The load-bearing citations I checked match the repository: workspace accessors and TreeRootForSpecDir behave as described; migrate is a prompt/inventory emitter rather than a transactional mover; panel and divergence still use repo-root review/ paths; contextpack, ownership, docsync, and bootstrap hardcode .mindspec/docs paths; glossary.md and policies.yml exist at the cited paths and have no non-test Go consumers under the requested grep. Impacted domain ownership manifests also match the cited package ownership. One non-blocking caveat: the cited ~246 literal occurrence count appears inflated under straightforward greps, though the blast-radius point remains directionally true.

findings.issue: The claim of roughly 246 literal .mindspec/docs/{specs,adr,domains,core} occurrences across 23 non-test Go files does not reproduce with the obvious repository grep. I found 99 exact slash-form matches across 21 non-test Go files under internal/cmd, and about 110 matches across 22 files when adding simple filepath.Join('.mindspec','docs',...) forms. This is not material to implementation because it still establishes a low-hundreds blast radius, but the statistic should be corrected when editing the spec.
